title: Split and defer non-critical JavaScript
description: Code-split by route and interaction, lazy-load below-the-fold and rarely-used modules, and keep the critical path bundle small.
body: Use when the initial JS payload is large or a route pulls in code it does not immediately need. Do not eagerly import heavy libraries (charts, editors, date pickers) into the critical path. Actions: route-level split, dynamic-import interaction-triggered features, preload only what the first paint needs, and inspect the bundle analyzer for the biggest chunks. Validation signals include a smaller initial transfer in the network panel and an improved LCP/TBT in a trace. Risk boundary: over-splitting causes request waterfalls and layout pop-in; verify perceived load did not get worse, not just that the main chunk shrank.
